incentive.statement: Target voice and causative source voice must each use one ordered recognized-input contract, preserve their intentional role distinction, and never authorize Active output from an unknown request.

coordinator.responsibility: Consolidate voice protocol recognition and shell parity without merging contextual allowed voices, source versus target roles, general Nawat voice mode, or ordered higher voice operations.

outcome: One core voice contract owns the ordered five-value target inventory and intentional three-value causative-source subset; application and surface requests fail closed on unknown values, while typed application-derived contextual inventories remain authoritative.

responsibilities.statement: Own target/source voice vocabularies, typed recognition, non-authorizing shell parity, and separation from higher voice-layer operations.

actors.contribution: Defined and registered target/source voice vocabulary, selection, and parity contracts while retaining typed contextual voice gates.

responsibilities.statement: Retain unknown target/source voice intent diagnostically and block before Active fallback can become generated authority.

actors.contribution: Reused the core voice contracts and blocked unknown target or source voice requests with empty results.

responsibilities.statement: Consume typed voice recognition, validate actual ordered shell/Canvas values, and pass invalid raw intent to the application gate.

actors.contribution: Removed handwritten renderer recognition, preserved invalid requests in blocked frames, and exposed ordered shell/Canvas parity diagnostics.

responsibilities.statement: Deliver the voice contract atomically and prove valid, invalid, poisoned, and contextual source/target behavior.

actors.contribution: Added unknown target/source application and surface proof, ordered shell/tag poison tests, cache entry 050, and live browser verification.

responsibilities.statement: Record the bounded voice slice and carry forward every unresolved comparison finding.

actors.contribution: Recorded scope, contextual exclusions, evidence, and remaining-risk status.

contracts.invariant: Only recognized target/source voice values enter contextual application authorization; source and target roles remain separate, and shell order remains stable for entrada v1 positional restoration.

retirements.removalEvidence: Unknown target/source application and renderer requests now return blocked frames with empty formula and surface, while valid Active remains authorized.

closure.detail: Unknown target or source voice can no longer normalize into authorized Active output, while the intentional role-specific voice subsets remain typed and separate.

evidence: Focused proof passed: VNC application 32/32, Lesson 11 plus continuity 26/26, matched UI 409/409, grammar-contract registry 43/43, and runtime manifest 75 modules plus 75 installers from one browser entry.

Hostile proof blocks fabricated target and causative-source voice requests before the Active layout fallback can authorize a formula or surface; poisoned shell values fail exact ordered Canvas parity.
Live browser entry 050 reported authorized five-value target and three-value source inventories, valid (nemi) Active formula #ni-0(nemi)0+0-0#, and no warning or error logs.

Alignment architecture metrics matched baseline exactly: responsibility actor 0/0, renderer construction 38/38, optional renderer dependency 0/0, registered-contract coverage 200/200, and browser scripts 1/1. The remaining hard-invariant failure is inherited board size and cross-action dirty-worktree overlap.
Lessons 20 through 22 remained 29/30 with only the pre-existing unrelated generated-source wording expectation failing; syntax, JSON parsing, and diff checks passed.
